sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.42 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Let$T_{d, c}$denote the class of$d$-generated, torsion-free nilpotent groups having class$c$. It is known that$T_{d, 2}$-groups are self-similar for all$d$, that$T_{2, 3}$-groups are self-similar, and that there are$T_{4, 3}$-groups that are not self-similar (A.\,Berlatto, T.\,Santos, Preprint, 2025, ). Are there$T_{3,3}$-groups that are not self-similar?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
